title: Lam Research: Buy This Pullback If You Missed Its July Bottom
text: LRCX has likely staged its medium-term bottom in July, as it suffered nearly a 50% collapse from its January highs. However, we are confident that its near-term headwinds have been de-risked. Notwithstanding, near-term challenges relating to its supply chain disruptions and worsening macros should persist. Furthermore, memory could see further weakness in H2'22.

title: Lam Research raises dividend by 15%
text: Lam Research Corp. LRCX, +3.28% said late Thursday its board of directors has approved 22.5 cent, or 15%, increase in the company's dividend, to $1.725 a share from $1.50 a share. The increase "is consistent with the intent for disciplined annual dividend growth," Lam Research said.

title: Lam Research Corporation Announces a 15% Increase in Quarterly Dividend
text: FREMONT, Calif., Aug. 25, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lam Research Corporation (Nasdaq: LRCX) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a $0.225, or 15%, increase in its quarterly dividend, from $1.50 to $1.725 per share of common stock. This increase is consistent with the intent for disciplined annual dividend growth. The dividend payment will be made October 5, 2022 to holders of record on September 14, 2022. Future dividend payments are subject to review and approval by the Board of Directors.

title: Lam Research, Applied Materials, KLA stocks drop after Micron says it will cut capital expenditures
text: Shares of chip-equipment companies Lam Research Corp. LRCX, -7.75% , Applied Materials Inc. AMAT, -6.93% and KLA Corp. KLAC, -6.59% are falling in Tuesday morning trading after Micron Technology Inc. MU, -5.31% said it would cut capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 more than it had previously indicated. Lam shares are down 7.2%, while Applied Materials shares are off 6.5% and KLA shares are dropping 6.1%.
